dark-chocolate-popcorn.name: Dark Chocolate Popcorn
instructions: Preheat oven to 121°C (120 degrees C). Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish.

Bring sugar, butter, cocoa powder, and corn syrup to a gentle boil in a saucepan; cook until thickened, 1 to 2 minutes. Stir in vanilla extract. Pour popcorn into a large bowl, add cocoa mixture and stir until popcorn is well coated. Spread evenly in prepared baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven, stirring occasionally, until mixture is set, about 30 minutes.
Remove from oven and cool to room temperature; break into clumps.

dark-chocolateorange-gingerbread-cake.name: Dark Chocolate–orange Gingerbread Cake

ingredients.item: Nonstick vegetable oil spray or room-temperature unsalted butter
. diamond crystal or  0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt
Diamond crystal or morton kosher salt

instructions: Place a rack in middle of oven and preheat to 350°. Line bottom of an 8"-diameter cake pan with a parchment paper round. Lightly coat parchment and pan with nonstick vegetable oil spray or room-temperature unsalted butter. Whisk 1½ cups (188 g) all-purpose flour, 1 tsp. Diamond Crystal or ½ tsp. Morton kosher salt, ½ tsp. baking powder, and ¼ tsp. baking soda in a medium bowl. Mix ½ cup mild-flavored (light) molasses and ½ cup water in a measuring glass.

Whisk ½ cup (packed; 100 g) dark brown sugar, ½ cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, room temperature, 1 Tbsp. finely grated ginger (from about a 3" piece), 1 Tbsp. finely grated orange zest (from 1 medium orange), 1 tsp. ground cinnamon, and ¼ tsp. freshly ground pepper in a medium bowl until light and creamy, about 5 minutes (if butter isn’t soft enough and mixture is hard to mix, smash butter with a rubber spatula at first to combine, then switch to the whisk). Add 1 large egg; whisk until emulsified, about 1 minute. Add dry ingredients in 3 additions, alternating with molasses mixture in 2 additions, beginning and ending with dry ingredients. Vigorously mix until combined, about 2 minutes (batter may look curdled and that’s okay).

Scrape batter into prepared pan and smooth surface with a small offset spatula or a spoon. Bake until cake springs back when gently pressed, 40–45 minutes. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let cake cool in pan 10 minutes. Run offset spatula around sides of pan, then invert cake out onto rack and let cool completely.

Place 3½–4 oz. 70% dark chocolate, chopped, in a medium bowl. Heat ½ cup heavy cream and ¼ tsp. Diamond Crystal or Morton kosher salt in a small saucepan over medium just until beginning to simmer; pour over chocolate. Let sit 2 minutes, then whisk gently until smooth. Let ganache cool until pourable, about 30 minutes. (Or, chill, stirring halfway through, until pourable, about 10 minutes.)

Spoon ganache over cake (leave upside down; this will give you a nice flat top) and, using offset spatula, spread evenly over surface, letting some drip down sides. Let cake sit until ganache is fully set, about 30 minutes. (Or, chill about 10 minutes.) Transfer to a cake stand or platter to serve.
Do Ahead: Cake can be baked 1 day ahead; store, airtight, at room temperature. Cake can be glazed 1 day ahead; store, covered with a cake dome or large bowl, at a cool room temperature.

delectable-prime-rib-au-jus.name: Delectable Prime Rib Au Jus
instructions: To make the roast: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Trim any thick pieces of dense, hard fat from roast, but leave smaller areas of fat in place.

Mix beef base and garlic together in a bowl. Mix thyme, oregano, peppercorns, and salt together in a separate bowl. Rub beef base mixture generously all over roast, then sprinkle with herb mixture. Place roast into a heavy-based roasting pan.

Roast in the preheated oven until meat has formed a brown crust, about 3 hours. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the roast should read 54°C (54 degrees C) for medium-rare. Remove from the oven and let stand while you make the sauce; the temperature will rise 5 to -12°C as it rests.

To make the au jus: Whisk water and beef base together in a saucepan over medium-low heat until base dissolves. Whisk in red wine, thyme, oregano, and garlic powder, then add onion, carrot, and celery. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer, stirring frequently, until sauce reduces by about half, 20 to 30 minutes. Strain au jus and discard vegetables. Serve with roast.

delicious-rigatoni-with-zucchini-and-lem.name: Delicious Rigatoni With Zucchini And Lemon
delicious-rigatoni-with-zucchini-and-lem.original_name: Delicioso rigatoni con calabacín y limón

Add salt and pepper and lemon frying. Pull back from the fire to stop the lemon from bitter.

Regive frying pan to low heat, add cream, butter and a chorrit from reserved water. Mix to form a light sauce.
Incorpora la pasta escurrida y revuelve para que absorba los sabores.
Add lemon juice and parmesan pitted. Removes until the salsa thickens and cover rigatoni evenly.
Sirve inmediatamente con hojas frescas de albahaca o menta y un toque extra de parmesano.

divine-potato-salad-with-fresh-cream-che.name: Divine Potato Salad With Fresh Cream Cheese
divine-potato-salad-with-fresh-cream-che.original_name: Θεική πατατοσαλάτα με φρέσκο τυρί κρέμα
instructions: Choose small fresh potatoes or baby potatoes and Wash them very well because we will also use the peel.
In building water add salt and lemon peel.
Optimally add 2-3 strands of herbs to flavor the potatoes. You can put dill.
Boil for 15-20’ until the potato softens and stings.
Remove them from building water and as warm as they are, cut them into small bites.
Immediately add salt pepper and cream cheese. Now that they're warm, they absorb all the injectors and flavors.
Add the mustard, lemon, vintagegar, pepper, cupcake pickles and onion.
Mix.
Finally sprinkle with the chopped herbs dill, parsley and some chives.
Serve warm as a meal or cold as a company.
It's a very good match for the crispy bacon as a flavor in the finish.

douro-style-stewed-octopus-teleculinaria.name: Douro-style Stewed Octopus
douro-style-stewed-octopus-teleculinaria.original_name: Polvo estufado à moda do Douro
instructions: Cut the octopus, still raw, into small pieces and boil them in water for a few minutes. Drain and reserve.
Make a braise with the olive oil, the laurel, the onion and the chopped garlic. Then add the octopus and let it cook a little.
Acrescente o tomate esmagado e deixe refogar. Regue com o vinho e deixe reduzir.
Add some water, season with salt and pepper, cover and let cooking until the octopus becomes soft. Serve sprinkled with chopped parsley and accompanied by cooked potatoes.

dried-cherry-and-cranberry-sauce.name: Dried Cherry And Cranberry Sauce
instructions: Stir the cranberry juice cocktail, water, brown sugar, cinnamon stick, and allspice together in a saucepan; bring to a boil. Add the cherries to the cranberry juice mixture; cook another 2 minutes. Add the cranberries; continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until the cranberries pop, about 10 minutes. Remove from heat and chill in refrigerator 8 hours to overnight. Remove and discard cinnamon stick before serving.

drunken-grapefruit-salad.name: Drunken Grapefruit Salad
instructions: Mix grapefruit, 1/2 cup reserved juice, and sugar in a bowl until sugar dissolves. Transfer grapefruit mixture to 8 serving cups; top each with about 1 teaspoon gin. Sprinkle minced mint over each cup. Garnish each cup with 1 mint leaf.
